item.description:
Aside 1.4.0

Transcripts now show who actually spoke, action items know their owner, and back-to-back meetings no longer trip over each other.

New

  • Real names in the transcript. When Aside can tell who is who from your calendar and the conversation, the transcript says "Anna" and "Mike" instead of "Speaker 1" and "Speaker 2" — in the clickable player and in the saved note.
  • Action items name their owner. Every item now leads with who it belongs to ("You: send the deck"), so a glance tells you what's yours.

Fixed

  • Back-to-back meetings work. Starting a new recording while the previous meeting was still being processed could silently break live transcription. The new recording now captures from the first second and the transcript catches up on its own.
  • Search results no longer stick to the top of the list.

Changed

  • A nicer segmented control. The Notes/Transcript switcher is a sliding pill now — clearer selection, smoother feel, everywhere it appears.
  • The Settings header sits properly centered.

If you're new here

  • No API key needed. Aside Cloud is the default: download, open, and it works. You can still use your own provider in Settings. (Cloud is invite-only while in testing, so a new install asks for an invite code once.)
  • The Aside window is home. Every meeting note, search, and tags in one place, plus what's next on your calendar with one click to join or record. Open it with ⌘⌥A or from the menu bar.
  • Keep it open during a meeting. Pin the panel so it stays up while you work, then click the ✕ to close it.

Anonymous usage counts (never your content) help guide what gets built next, with an off switch in Settings. Crash reports can be shared too, opt-in and technical only, never audio or transcripts.
